hero.subtitle: StudaFly transforms your study abroad preparation into a structured and personalised journey. No more stress, just organisation!

features.title: All the features you need
features.subtitle: A complete tool to turn your preparation into a smooth, stress-free experience
features.timeline_desc: Automatic deadlines tailored to your destination and departure date. Never miss an important step again.
features.checklist_desc: A personalised task list adapted to your destination country and mobility type (Erasmus, internship, etc.).
features.budget_desc: Estimate your budget accurately using feedback from former students at your destination.
features.guide_desc: Local transport, tips, accommodation... All the practical info to get your stay off to a great start.
features.documents_desc: Store all your important documents securely, accessible even offline.
features.notifications_desc: Receive reminders for all critical deadlines. Always stay on track.

how_it_works.title: How does it work?
how_it_works.subtitle: Three simple steps to start your adventure
how_it_works.step1_desc: Enter your destination, departure date and mobility type in just a few clicks.
how_it_works.step2_desc: Receive your personalised action plan with all the key steps of your preparation.
how_it_works.step3_desc: Enjoy your experience abroad with complete peace of mind — everything is under control!

destinations.title: Popular Destinations
destinations.subtitle: Over 30 countries covered with detailed information

cta.title: Ready for takeoff?
cta.subtitle: Join thousands of students who have simplified their preparation with StudaFly. Start for free today!
cta.no_card: No credit card required • Set up in 2 minutes

login.subtitle: Log in to continue your preparation

login.side_subtitle: Your personalized timeline is waiting for you

register.side_subtitle: Transform your mobility preparation into a structured journey

register.subtitle: Start your preparation in minutes

step3.prompt: Add a profile picture or choose an emoji to personalize your space

empty.title: No tasks in this category
empty.subtitle: Add a task with the + button

modal.title: Add a task
modal.title_placeholder: E.g. Open a local bank account

t_1.title: Visa application
t_1.description: Submit the visa application to the relevant consulate.

t_2.title: University acceptance letter
t_2.description: Obtain the official confirmation from the host university.

t_3.title: Valid passport (+6 months)
t_3.description: Check the expiry date of your passport.

t_4.title: International bank card
t_4.description: Apply for a no-fee card for abroad (e.g. Wise, Revolut).

t_5.title: 6-month budget plan
t_5.description: Set a monthly budget based on local cost of living.

t_6.title: EHIC (European Health Insurance Card)
t_6.description: Apply for your European Health Insurance Card (EU only).

t_7.title: Essential medication stock
t_7.description: Build up a sufficient supply for the duration of your stay.

t_8.title: Signed housing contract
t_8.description: Obtain a lease or university residence confirmation.

t_9.title: Housing inventory
t_9.description: Carry out a joint inventory on the day you move in.

t_10.title: Local SIM or international plan
t_10.description: Subscribe to a mobile plan suited to your destination.

t_11.title: Electrical adapter
t_11.description: Check local electrical standards and buy the right adapter.

header.subtitle: {{count}} steps to prepare your mobility with peace of mind

six-months-before.label: 6 months before departure
six-months-before.description: Lay the administrative and financial foundations

e1.title: Erasmus / grant application
e1.description: Submit your application to your home institution before the deadline.

e2.title: Housing search
e2.description: Start searching (student residence, flat share, temporary Airbnb). Earlier is better.

e3.title: Overall budget planning
e3.description: Estimate costs (housing, food, transport, leisure) for the entire stay.

e4.title: Visa application
e4.description: Compile and submit the visa application to the consulate if required for your destination.

e5.title: Sign housing contract
e5.description: Finalise and sign the lease or confirm your student residence booking.

e6.title: EHIC application
e6.description: Apply for the European Health Insurance Card (processing time: 2 to 4 weeks).

e7.title: Open international bank account
e7.description: Get a no-fee card for abroad (Wise, Revolut, N26) to avoid commissions.

e8.title: Book flight
e8.description: Compare and book your outbound (and return if possible) flight in advance for better fares.

e9.title: Essential medication stock
e9.description: Prepare a travel pharmacy suited to your destination and stay length.

e10.title: Travel insurance
e10.description: Check and top up your insurance cover abroad if the EHIC is not sufficient.

e11.title: Pack your bags
e11.description: Make a list and pack without exceeding baggage weight limits.

e12.title: Enrol at host university
e12.description: Complete administrative registration and collect your student card and digital access.

e13.title: Buy local SIM card
e13.description: Subscribe to a local mobile plan to avoid roaming charges.

e14.title: Housing inventory
e14.description: Carry out a joint inventory check-in and photograph it to protect your deposit.

e15.title: Explore the neighbourhood and campus
e15.description: Locate shops, transport links and key places for your daily life.
